questions.prompt: Most common cause of hydration mismatch errors?

choices.text: Server-rendered HTML differs from what React tries to render during client hydration. Triggers: using `Date.now()`, `Math.random()`, or `new Date()` directly in render (different value server vs client); accessing `window`/`localStorage`/`navigator` in code paths that also run on the server; CSS-in-JS not properly configured for SSR; browser extensions modifying HTML. Fix: move client-only logic into useEffect so it runs only after hydration; for genuinely-different content like timestamps, use `suppressHydrationWarning`; for theme switching, use libraries like next-themes that handle the SSR case properly.

questions.explanation: Server/client divergence — Date.now, random, window access. Move to useEffect.

questions.prompt: Production build fails on Vercel but works locally. Common causes?

choices.text: Multiple possibilities to check: (1) Node version mismatch — pin via `"engines": { "node": "20.x" }` in package.json so local and Vercel use the same version; (2) Case sensitivity — macOS dev is case-insensitive but Linux production is case-sensitive, so `import './Foo'` works locally with `foo.tsx` but fails on Linux; (3) Missing committed files — something in .gitignore that's needed at build time; (4) Different env vars — variables set locally in .env.local but not configured in Vercel Production scope. Reproduce by running `npm run build` on a clean checkout in a Linux environment (Docker locally or Vercel preview).

questions.explanation: Node version, case sensitivity, missing files, or env vars not in production scope.
